questions.question: What is the name of the white keys pattern that repeats on a piano?
questions.explanation: An octave is the interval between one musical pitch and another with double its frequency. The pattern of white and black keys repeats every octave.

questions.question: How many black keys are there in one octave?
questions.explanation: There are 5 black keys in each octave, representing the sharp and flat notes between the natural notes.

questions.question: What does 'Middle C' refer to?
options: The C key in the middle of the keyboard
The first C key on the left
questions.explanation: Middle C (C4) is the C note located near the middle of a standard 88-key piano keyboard, and serves as a reference point.

questions.question: What is a chord?
options: Three or more notes played together
questions.explanation: A chord is a group of three or more notes played simultaneously, creating harmony.

questions.question: What does 'tempo' mean in music?
questions.explanation: Tempo refers to the speed or pace at which a piece of music is played, usually measured in beats per minute (BPM).
